violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. (Per the PIC, previous manager left position.) New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The cabinetry along the back wall was observed to have an accumulation of residue, droppings, and debris inside. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Milk in the walk-in cooler was observed held at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

Boxes of single service items were observed stored on the floor in the backroom area. Single service items are to be stored of the floor to prevent possible contamination by the physical facilities. (Items were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC due to contact with pooling water in the backroom area.) New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

At the time of inspection, the walk-in cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit with milk observed at 43-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, a delivery was recently added and door remained open for a while. Unit is to be monitored and adjusted/serviced for proper cold holding temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

At the time of inspection, the facility was observed to have an accumulation of water and debris on the floor in the backroom area. At the time of inspection, employees began cleaning and sanitizing the floor with a bleach and water mixture. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025
The lowboy and reach-in unit were observed with elevated ambient temperatures. Both units were not in use. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/04/2024

Various foods were observed held at 42-44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All food was relocated to walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023

The lowboy and reach-in refrigerator was not able to hold an adequate ambient temperature. All food inside was observed at an elevated temperature. Units to not be used for TCS foods until serviced/repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the 3-bay. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 12/25/2023
The 3-bay sink was observed with a small leak in the sanitizing bay of the 3-bay sink. 3-bay sink to be repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2023
